km.Henham, Suffolk, England: ហេនហាម, ស៊ូfប៉ូលក, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Richard Crane於1180年出生在英國素福爾可亨哈姆。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1218年在他的家鄉享年38歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Crane于1180年出生在英国素福爾可亨哈姆。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1218年在他的家乡享年38岁。

nameMeanings.name: Crane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname, most likely for a tall, thin man with long legs, from Middle English cran ‘crane’ (the bird), Old English cran, cron. The term included the heron until the introduction of a separate word for the latter in the 14th century.

variants.name: Craine
meanings: Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac (Giolla) Ciaráin ‘son of (the devotee of) Ciarán’, a personal name based on a diminutive of Gaelic ciar, Manx keeir ‘black’. The place Ballacraine on the Isle of Man means ‘Craine's farm’.

variants.name: Corn
meanings: English: nickname from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ).
English: metonymic occupational name for a maker or user of hand mills, Old English cweorn.
Americanized form of German, Dutch, Czech, or Jewish   Korn  , or a shortened form of any of the composite names formed with this element.

variants.name: Crank
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): from Middle English cranke ‘lively, lusty, vigorous’, hence a nickname for a cheerful, boisterous, or merry person.
English: possibly in some cases, a nickname from cranuc, a diminutive of Middle English cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ).

Possibly an Americanized form of German Kranke: from Low German Kraneke ‘crane’, applied to someone thought to resemble the bird in some way, or a nickname for a poor physical specimen, from Middle High German kranc ‘sickly, ailing’.

variants.name: Cran
meanings: Dutch: variant of Kraan or one of its variants, such as Craane and Krane .

variants.name: Cornforth
meanings: English (North Yorkshire and Durham): habitational name from Cornforth in County Durham, named with Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ (see Crane ) + ford ‘ford’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Crane was born in 1180, in Henham, Suffolk, England. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1218, in his hometown, at the age of 38.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Crane est né en 1180, à Henham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1218, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 38 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Crane wurde 1180 in Henham, Suffolk, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1218 im Alter von 38 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Crane è nato il nel 1180 nel Henham, Suffolk, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1218, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 38 anni.

ja.Henham, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクヘンハム
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Craneは1180年にイギリススフォルクヘンハムで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1218年に故郷で38歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Crane(은)는 1180년 영국 수폴크 헨함에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1218년 그의 고향에서 38세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Crane nasceu em 1180, em Henham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1218, em sua cidade natal, com 38 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Crane родился в 1180 г. в местности хенам, суффолк, Англия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1218 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 38 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Crane nació en 1180, en Henham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1218, en su pueblo, a la edad de 38 años.
